attachment.content:
Powered by Entire:
  This conversation will be linked to your next commit.
  3 other active conversation(s) in this workspace will also be included.
  Use 'entire status' for more information.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
- lightfast-changelog: Write and review AEO-optimized changelog entries for Lightfast. Triggers when the user asks
to write a changelog entry, review changelog content, or asks about changelog structure,
AEO optimization, or release notes formatting.
- lightfast-sdk: Use Lightfast tools to search across team knowledge and interact with connected providers
(GitHub, Linear, Sentry, Vercel, Apollo). Triggers when the user asks about errors, issues,
PRs, deployments, or wants to search across their dev tools. Also triggers proactively when
investigating bugs, reviewing code, or checking deployment status.
- lightfast-inngest: Inspect Inngest function runs, debug failures, send events, and invoke functions via the
local Inngest dev server MCP. Triggers when the user asks about background jobs, workflow
runs, failed steps, event delivery, or wants to invoke/test Inngest functions.
- lightfast-db: Query and inspect the Postgres database schema and data. Triggers when the user asks about
tables, columns, rows, database schema, wants to run a SELECT query, or needs to understand
the data model. Read-only — cannot modify data.
- turborepo: Turborepo monorepo build system guidance. Triggers on: turbo.json, task pipelines,
dependsOn, caching, remote cache, the "turbo" CLI, --filter, --affected, CI optimization, environment
variables, internal packages, monorepo structure/best practices, and boundaries.

Use when user: configures tasks/workflows/pipelines, creates packages, sets up
monorepo, shares code between apps, runs changed/affected packages, debugs cache,
or has apps/packages directories.
- implement_plan: Implement technical plans from thoughts/shared/plans with verification
- create_plan: Create detailed implementation plans through interactive research and iteration
- validate_plan: Validate implementation against plan, verify success criteria, identify issues
- commit: Create git commits with user approval and no Claude attribution
- oneshot_merge: Commit changes, create PR, and merge into main in one shot
- improve_plan: Adversarial review of implementation plans — find weaknesses, kill code smells, improve DX
- research_codebase: Document codebase as-is with thoughts directory for historical context
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- statusline: Set up Claude Code's status line UI
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
- insights: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions
- team-onboarding: Help teammates ramp on Claude Code with a guide from your usage

content.text:
Big pile — roughly 8 logical groups worth separating into commits:

**Features (new)**
- GitHub org binding — `api/app/src/router/org/github-binding.ts`, `api/platform/src/lib/github-binding.ts`, `apps/app/src/lib/github-binding.ts`, `db/app/src/schema/tables/github-org-bindings.ts`, migration `0064_loose_nightmare.sql`, `github-connect-step.tsx`, onboard flow, plus touches in `api/platform/oauth/callback.ts`, `api/app/router/user/organization.ts`, `apps/app/src/app/(providers)/provider/github/connected/page.tsx`
- Workspace layout rework — `(workspace)/layout.tsx`, `_components/`, `workspace-header.tsx`, `onboard-header.tsx`, sidebar/team-switcher changes

**Content / marketing (www)**
- Changelog v010 rename + featured images — new mdx, deleted old mdx, `v010-sdk-mcp` + `v010-sources` png/webp, two new Remotion compositions, `Root.tsx`/`manifest.ts`, `builders/changelog.ts`, changelog layout/page
- Integrations branded MCP cards — `mcp-bento.tsx`, integrations layout, `integration-icons.tsx`
- Landing/pricing/faq/legal/nav tweaks

**Removals**
- `packages/app-vercel/*` (whole package deleted)
- `account/teams/invite/*` flow deleted
- Old `2026-03-26-initial.mdx` changelog

**Infra / config**
- `.mcp.json`, `knip.json`, `apps/platform/turbo.json`, `apps/app/microfrontends.json`, `proxy.ts`, env files
- DB schema index/relations/tables plumbing for the new table

**Providers / validation**
- `packages/app-providers/*` GitHub provider updates (api, auth, schemas, transformers, categories, display, event-labels)
- `packages/app-validation/src/schemas/entities.ts`

**Thoughts (docs-only, safe to batch)**
- ~30 plans + ~25 research docs in `thoughts/shared/`

**Local / untracked oddities to review before staging**
- `--fullpage` (stray file at repo root — likely a misfired flag, probably delete)
- `.claude/projects/` and `.claude/skills/lightfast-changelog/` — confirm whether these belong in the repo or are local-only

Want me to stage + commit these in that order, or pick a subset first? I'd start by cleaning up `--fullpage` and confirming the `.claude/` dirs before anything else.

message.content: hmm focus on content marketting. nothing that touches apps/app basically.

content.text:
Filtering to www + related (no apps/app, no api, no db), here's what's left and how I'd split it:

**Group 1 — Changelog v010 (rename + featured images)**
- `apps/www/src/content/changelog/2026-03-26-initial.mdx` (deleted)
- `apps/www/src/content/changelog/2026-03-26-lightfast-engineering-intelligence-shipped.mdx` (new)
- `apps/www/public/images/changelog/v010-{sdk-mcp,sources}.{png,webp}`
- `packages/app-remotion/src/compositions/changelog-v010-sdk-mcp/`
- `packages/app-remotion/src/compositions/changelog-v010-sources/`
- `packages/app-remotion/src/{Root.tsx,manifest.ts}`
- `apps/www/src/lib/builders/changelog.ts`
- `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(marketing)/(content)/changelog/{layout.tsx,page.tsx}`

**Group 2 — Integrations branded MCP cards**
- `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(marketing)/(content)/integrations/_components/mcp-bento.tsx`
- `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(marketing)/(content)/integrations/layout.tsx`
- `packages/ui/src/components/integration-icons.tsx`

**Group 3 — Marketing page tweaks**
- `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(marketing)/(landing)/page.tsx`
- `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(marketing)/(content)/pricing/page.tsx`
- `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(marketing)/legal/layout.tsx`
- `apps/www/src/app/(app)/_components/faq-section.tsx`
- `apps/www/src/config/nav.ts`

**Group 4 — Docs/API-ref builders**
- `apps/www/src/lib/builders/api-reference.ts`
- `apps/www/src/lib/builders/docs.ts`

Groups 1–3 are clearly content/marketing. Group 4 is borderline — builders feed docs which are content but the change might be incidental. Want me to diff each group so we can write tight messages, or just run `/commit` on Group 1 first?

# Commit Changes

You are tasked with creating git commits for the changes made during this session.

## Process:

1. **Think about what changed:**
   - Review the conversation history and understand what was accomplished
   - Run `git status` to see current changes
   - Run `git diff` to understand the modifications
   - Consider whether changes should be one commit or multiple logical commits

2. **Plan your commit(s):**
   - Identify which files belong together
   - Draft clear, descriptive commit messages
   - Use imperative mood in commit messages
   - Focus on why the changes were made, not just what

3. **Present your plan to the user:**
   - List the files you plan to add for each commit
   - Show the commit message(s) you'll use
   - Ask: "I plan to create [N] commit(s) with these changes. Shall I proceed?"

4. **Execute upon confirmation:**
   - Use `git add` with specific files (never use `-A` or `.`)
   - Create commits with your planned messages
   - Show the result with `git log --oneline -n [number]`

## Remember:

- You have the full context of what was done in this session
- Group related changes together
- Keep commits focused and atomic when possible
- The user trusts your judgment - they asked you to commit
Let me peek at each group's diffs to write accurate messages.

content.text:
Here's the commit plan. Splitting into 6 atomic commits:

**1. `feat(www): publish v010 changelog entry with Remotion featured images`**
- `apps/www/src/content/changelog/2026-03-26-initial.mdx` (delete)
- `apps/www/src/content/changelog/2026-03-26-lightfast-engineering-intelligence-shipped.mdx` (new)
- `apps/www/public/images/changelog/v010-{sdk-mcp,sources}.{png,webp}`
- `packages/app-remotion/src/compositions/changelog-v010-{sdk-mcp,sources}/`
- `packages/app-remotion/src/{Root.tsx,manifest.ts}`

**2. `refactor(www): simplify changelog layout and expand FAQ`**
- `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(marketing)/(content)/changelog/{layout,page}.tsx`

**3. `feat(www): add branded MCP bento to integrations page`**
- `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(marketing)/(content)/integrations/_components/mcp-bento.tsx`
- `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(marketing)/(content)/integrations/layout.tsx`
- `packages/ui/src/components/integration-icons.tsx`

**4. `refactor(www): rework landing hero with founder-letter card`**
- `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(marketing)/(landing)/page.tsx`
- `apps/www/src/config/nav.ts` (move Integrations into Resources dropdown)

**5. `style(www): add legal page bottom border and formatter tweaks`**
- `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(marketing)/legal/layout.tsx`
- `apps/www/src/app/(app)/(marketing)/(content)/pricing/page.tsx`
- `apps/www/src/app/(app)/_components/faq-section.tsx`

**6. `feat(www): include jobTitle in schema.org Person across SEO builders`**
- `apps/www/src/lib/builders/{api-reference,changelog,docs}.ts`

I plan to create 6 commits with these changes. Shall I proceed?
